title: Limit theorems for random walks with spatio-temporal drift
authors: Ngo P. N. Ngoc, Tuan-Minh Nguyen
abstract: We study a class of discrete-time random walks in $\mathbb{R}^d$ whose conditional drift decays polynomially in time and grows polynomially with the distance from the origin to the current position. This class is related to several models of self-interacting random processes. We determine the asymptotic behavior of the walk under the assumption that its increments have moments of order $p$ for some $p>2$. In the linear case, where the drift depends linearly on the current position, we establish a phase transition in the convergence in distribution of the normalized process to Gaussian limits. In the nonlinear case, we identify three distinct regimes separated by a critical line and show that the normalized process exhibits qualitatively different behaviors in each regime, including convergence in distribution to a Gaussian law, convergence to a non-Gaussian limit given by the stationary distribution of a stochastic differential equation, and almost sure localization on a hypersphere.
summary: This paper provides a complete classification of the long-term behavior of random walks with complex, time-dependent, and position-dependent drifts. It identifies how varying the strength of the drift relative to noise leads to transitions between diffusive, SDE-driven, and localized states.
main_problem: The paper investigates the asymptotic behavior and limit theorems for a class of discrete-time random walks in R^d characterized by spatio-temporal drift.
main_result: The authors establish a phase transition in the convergence of the normalized process, identifying three distinct regimes in the nonlinear case: Gaussian convergence, convergence to an SDE-based stationary distribution, and almost sure localization on a hypersphere.
method: The study employs martingale theory, multivariate martingale central limit theorems, Lyapunov functions, and diffusion approximations to analyze the process.
model_or_system: A discrete-time random walk in R^d where the conditional drift decays polynomially in time and grows polynomially with the distance from the origin, related to models like the Elephant Random Walk and self-interacting processes.
key_observables: The asymptotic distribution of the scaled position (normalized process), the scaling exponent gamma, and the asymptotic covariance matrix.
important_parameters: Spatial scaling exponent (alpha), temporal scaling exponent (beta), and the eigenvalues of the drift matrix (A).
main_assumptions: Increments must have moments of order p > 2; the drift field may be non-Lipschitz at the origin; the covariance matrix Sigma is assumed to be positive definite.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the RWSD model and its regimes, followed by proofs for the nonlinear case (Theorem 1.1) and the linear case (Theorem 1.2/1.3), and concludes with an appendix on non-convergence and martingale CLT tools.
why_interesting: The study of phase transitions and localization in stochastic processes with memory and spatio-temporal dependence is highly relevant to understanding non-equilibrium dynamics and many-body localization in open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
